title: torilis anthriscus
intro: Torilis anthriscus, also known as hemlock parsley or cow parsley, is a biennial flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, soothing digestive problems, and reducing fever. Torilis anthriscus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being.
